content.content:
1→# Testing Best Practices — Chat Exporter
     2→
     3→> Verbindliche Referenz für alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
     4→> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** — es enthält die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.
     5→
     6→## Tech-Stack
     7→
     8→| Bereich | Tool | Version |
     9→|---------|------|---------|
    10→| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
    11→| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
    12→| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
    13→| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | — |
    14→| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |
    15→
    16→## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing
    17→
    18→**Lies nur die Module, die für deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**
    19→
    20→| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
    21→|---------|-------------------|
    22→| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
    23→| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
    24→| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
    25→| **Code-Review durchführen** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
    26→| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
    27→| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
    28→| **Test-Kategorien wählen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
    29→| **Verfügbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gültig)
    34→
    35→### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung
    36→
    37→Tests prüfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.
    38→
    39→```typescript
    40→// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
    41→test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
    42→  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })
    43→
    44→  const result = generatePreview(selection)
    45→
    46→  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
    47→  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
    48→})
    49→
    50→// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
    51→test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
    52→  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
    53→  generatePreview(createSelection())
    54→  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
    55→})
    56→```
    57→
    58→### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)
    59→
    60→Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:
    61→
    62→```typescript
    63→test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
    64→  // Arrange
    65→  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }
    66→
    67→  // Act
    68→  const result = createSelection(overrides)
    69→
    70→  // Assert
    71→  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
    72→  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
    73→  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
    74→})
    75→```
    76→
    77→### Given-When-Then (für komplexe Szenarien)
    78→
    79→```typescript
    80→test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
    81→  /**
    82→   * Given: Eine aktive Session für importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
    83→   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
    84→   * Then: Bestehende Session zurückgegeben, keine neue erstellt
    85→   */
    86→})
    87→```
    88→
    89→### Tests als Dokumentation
    90→
    91→Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
    92→- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
    93→- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
    94→- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)
    99→
   100→| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
   101→|--------------|-------------|
   102→| Implementierungsdetails testen | Prüfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
   103→| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | Prüfe spezifische Werte |
   104→| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo möglich |
   105→| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | Prüfe nur Schema-Konformität |
   106→| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
   107→| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
   108→| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) für Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)
   113→
   114→| Element | Format | Beispiel |
   115→|---------|--------|----------|
   116→| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
   117→| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
   118→| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
   119→| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
   120→| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
   121→| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
   122→| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Detail-Module
   127→
   128→| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
   129→|-------|------|--------|
   130→| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
   131→| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
   132→| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
   133→| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
   134→| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
   135→| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
   136→| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
   137→| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, Ausführungsbefehle |
   138→| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, häufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Änderungen an Test-Praktiken müssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.
   143→

1→import { z } from "zod";
     2→
     3→import { blockSchema, roleSchema } from "./conversation.js";
     4→
     5→export const importSnapshotMetadataSchema = z.object({
     6→  articleCount: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
     7→  messageCount: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
     8→  rawHtmlBytes: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
     9→});
    10→
    11→export const normalizedSnapshotStructuringSchema = z.object({
    12→  status: z.enum(["disabled", "skipped", "applied", "partial", "failed"]),
    13→  provider: z.string(),
    14→  model: z.string().optional(),
    15→  candidateCount: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
    16→  attemptedCount: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
    17→  repairedCount: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
    18→  failedCount: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
    19→  skippedCount: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
    20→  skippedReason: z.string().optional(),
    21→});
    22→
    23→export const normalizedSnapshotParserSchema = z.object({
    24→  source: z.string().optional(),
    25→  blockCount: z.number().int().nonnegative().optional(),
    26→  usedFallback: z.boolean().optional(),
    27→  strategy: z.enum(["deterministic", "ai-repair", "fallback"]).optional(),
    28→  model: z.string().optional(),
    29→});
    30→
    31→export const normalizedSnapshotMessageSchema = z.object({
    32→  id: z.string(),
    33→  role: roleSchema,
    34→  blocks: z.array(blockSchema),
    35→  rawText: z.string().optional(),
    36→  rawHtml: z.string().optional(),
    37→  parser: normalizedSnapshotParserSchema.optional(),
    38→});
    39→
    40→export const normalizedSnapshotPayloadSchema = z.object({
    41→  title: z.string(),
    42→  messages: z.array(normalizedSnapshotMessageSchema),
    43→  warnings: z.array(z.string()),
    44→  structuring: normalizedSnapshotStructuringSchema.optional(),
    45→});
    46→
    47→export const importSnapshotSchema = z.object({
    48→  importId: z.string(),
    49→  sourceUrl: z.string().url(),
    50→  finalUrl: z.string().url(),
    51→  fetchedAt: z.string(),
    52→  pageTitle: z.string(),
    53→  rawHtmlBytes: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
    54→  rawHtmlPreview: z.string(),
    55→  rawHtmlTruncated: z.boolean(),
    56→  normalizedPayload: normalizedSnapshotPayloadSchema,
    57→  fetchMetadata: importSnapshotMetadataSchema,
    58→});
    59→
    60→export type ImportSnapshot = z.infer<typeof importSnapshotSchema>;
    61→export type ImportSnapshotMetadata = z.infer<
    62→  typeof importSnapshotMetadataSchema
    63→>;
    64→export type NormalizedSnapshotMessage = z.infer<
    65→  typeof normalizedSnapshotMessageSchema
    66→>;
    67→export type NormalizedSnapshotParser = z.infer<
    68→  typeof normalizedSnapshotParserSchema
    69→>;
    70→export type NormalizedSnapshotPayload = z.infer<
    71→  typeof normalizedSnapshotPayloadSchema
    72→>;
    73→export type NormalizedSnapshotStructuring = z.infer<
    74→  typeof normalizedSnapshotStructuringSchema
    75→>;
    76→

input.prompt:
Implement Step 2 of the Kontext-Editor plan: DB Schema for new tables conversationSnapshots and messageEdits.

Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor.
Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`).

## Context

Working directory: /Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter

The DB is at `apps/server/src/db/`. Uses:
- `better-sqlite3` with `drizzle-orm`
- Schema definitions in `schema.ts` using `sqliteTable`
- DDL in `client.ts` using `sqlite.exec()`
- Pattern: schema.ts defines Drizzle tables, client.ts has raw CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS

### Existing schema.ts pattern:
```typescript
import { index, sqliteTable, text, uniqueIndex } from "drizzle-orm/sqlite-core";

export const messageDeletions = sqliteTable(
  "message_deletions",
  {
    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    importId: text("import_id").notNull().references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    messageId: text("message_id").notNull(),
    reason: text("reason"),
    deletedAt: text("deleted_at").notNull(),
  },
  (table) => [
    uniqueIndex("idx_message_deletions_import_message").on(table.importId, table.messageId),
  ],
);
```

### Existing client.ts pattern:
DDL statements are added in `sqlite.exec()` block. Add new CREATE TABLE statements.

Also add `integer` import from `drizzle-orm/sqlite-core` for `isActive`.

1→import fs from "node:fs";
     2→import path from "node:path";
     3→import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
     4→
     5→import Database from "better-sqlite3";
     6→import { drizzle } from "drizzle-orm/better-sqlite3";
     7→
     8→import "../load-env.js";
     9→import * as schema from "./schema.js";
    10→
    11→const defaultDbPath = fileURLToPath(
    12→  new URL("../../../../data/chat-exporter.db", import.meta.url),
    13→);
    14→
    15→export const databasePath =
    16→  process.env.CHAT_EXPORTER_DB_PATH &&
    17→  process.env.CHAT_EXPORTER_DB_PATH.trim().length > 0
    18→    ? path.resolve(process.env.CHAT_EXPORTER_DB_PATH)
    19→    : defaultDbPath;
    20→
    21→fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(databasePath), { recursive: true });
    22→
    23→const sqlite = new Database(databasePath);
    24→
    25→sqlite.pragma("journal_mode = WAL");
    26→sqlite.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");
    27→sqlite.pragma("busy_timeout = 5000");
    28→
    29→export const db = drizzle(sqlite, { schema });
    30→export const rawDb = sqlite;
    31→
    32→export function withTransaction<T>(fn: () => T): T {
    33→  return rawDb.transaction(fn)();
    34→}
    35→
    36→// Note: sqlite.exec is the better-sqlite3 API for DDL — no shell invocation.
    37→sqlite.exec(`
    38→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS imports (
    39→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    40→    source_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    41→    source_platform TEXT NOT NULL,
    42→    mode TEXT NOT NULL,
    43→    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    44→    current_stage TEXT NOT NULL,
    45→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    46→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    47→    warnings_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
    48→    error TEXT,
    49→    summary_json TEXT,
    50→    conversation_json TEXT,
    51→    artifacts_json TEXT
    52→  );
    53→
    54→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_imports_created_at ON imports (created_at DESC);
    55→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_imports_status ON imports (status);
    56→
    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS import_snapshots (
    58→    import_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    59→    source_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    60→    final_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    61→    fetched_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    62→    page_title TEXT NOT NULL,
    63→    raw_html TEXT NOT NULL,
    64→    normalized_payload_json TEXT NOT NULL,
    65→    fetch_metadata_json TEXT NOT NULL,
    66→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
    67→  );
    68→
    69→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS adjustment_sessions (
    70→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    71→    import_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    72→    target_format TEXT NOT NULL,
    73→    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    74→    selection_json TEXT NOT NULL,
    75→    preview_artifact_json TEXT,
    76→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    77→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
    78→  );
    79→
    80→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_adjustment_sessions_import_id
    81→    ON adjustment_sessions (import_id, created_at DESC);
    82→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_adjustment_sessions_target_format
    83→    ON adjustment_sessions (target_format, updated_at DESC);
    84→
    85→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS adjustment_messages (
    86→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    87→    session_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES adjustment_sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    88→    role TEXT NOT NULL,
    89→    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    90→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL
    91→  );
    92→
    93→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_adjustment_messages_session_id
    94→    ON adjustment_messages (session_id, created_at ASC);
    95→
    96→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS format_rules (
    97→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    98→    import_id TEXT REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    99→    target_format TEXT NOT NULL,
   100→    kind TEXT NOT NULL,
   101→    scope TEXT NOT NULL,
   102→    status TEXT NOT NULL,
   103→    selector_json TEXT NOT NULL,
   104→    instruction TEXT NOT NULL,
   105→    compiled_rule_json TEXT,
   106→    source_session_id TEXT REFERENCES adjustment_sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   107→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   108→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
   109→  );
   110→
   111→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_import_id
   112→    ON format_rules (import_id, target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   113→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_profile
   114→    ON format_rules (target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   115→

   118→    import_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   119→    session_id TEXT REFERENCES adjustment_sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,

   121→    target_format TEXT NOT NULL,
   122→    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
   123→    payload_json TEXT,

   125→  );

   129→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_adjustment_events_session_id
   130→    ON adjustment_events (session_id, created_at DESC);
   131→

   134→    import_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   135→    message_id TEXT NOT NULL,
   136→    reason TEXT,
   137→    deleted_at TEXT NOT NULL

   143→
   144→// Migration: make format_rules.import_id nullable (SQLite cannot ALTER COLUMN)
   145→// Uses a table-rebuild approach because SQLite does not support DROP NOT NULL.
   146→const importIdColumn = sqlite
   147→  .prepare(
   148→    `SELECT "notnull" FROM pragma_table_info('format_rules') WHERE name = 'import_id'`,
   149→  )
   150→  .get() as { notnull: number } | undefined;
   151→
   152→if (importIdColumn && importIdColumn.notnull === 1) {
   153→  // Per SQLite docs, PRAGMA foreign_keys is a no-op inside a transaction, so it
   154→  // must be issued outside one. We therefore split the migration into three
   155→  // separate sqlite.exec calls:
   156→  //   1. Pragma off  (outside transaction)
   157→  //   2. DDL block wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT — crash-safe: a failure between
   158→  //      DROP TABLE and RENAME rolls back automatically, preserving all data.
   159→  //   3. Pragma on   (outside transaction)
   160→  sqlite.exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF;");
   161→
   162→  sqlite.exec(`
   163→    BEGIN;
   164→
   165→    -- Create the new table with nullable import_id
   166→    CREATE TABLE format_rules_new (
   167→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   168→      import_id TEXT REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   169→      target_format TEXT NOT NULL,
   170→      kind TEXT NOT NULL,
   171→      scope TEXT NOT NULL,
   172→      status TEXT NOT NULL,
   173→      selector_json TEXT NOT NULL,
   174→      instruction TEXT NOT NULL,
   175→      compiled_rule_json TEXT,
   176→      source_session_id TEXT REFERENCES adjustment_sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   177→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   178→      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
   179→    );
   180→
   181→    -- Copy data from old table to new
   182→    INSERT INTO format_rules_new SELECT * FROM format_rules;
   183→
   184→    -- Drop original and rename new to final name
   185→    DROP TABLE format_rules;
   186→    ALTER TABLE format_rules_new RENAME TO format_rules;
   187→
   188→    -- Recreate indexes
   189→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_import_id
   190→      ON format_rules (import_id, target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   191→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_profile
   192→      ON format_rules (target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   193→
   194→    COMMIT;
   195→  `);
   196→
   197→  sqlite.exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;");
   198→
   199→  // Verify FK integrity after migration
   200→  const fkErrors = sqlite.pragma("foreign_key_check") as unknown[];
   201→  if (fkErrors.length > 0) {
   202→    throw new Error(
   203→      `FK-Integritätsprüfung nach Migration fehlgeschlagen: ${JSON.stringify(fkErrors)}`,
   204→    );
   205→  }
   206→}
   207→
   208→// Migration: add error_stage column to imports table
   209→// Note: sqlite.exec is the better-sqlite3 DDL API — no shell invocation.
   210→const errorStageColumn = sqlite
   211→  .prepare(
   212→    `SELECT name FROM pragma_table_info('imports') WHERE name = 'error_stage'`,
   213→  )
   214→  .get() as { name: string } | undefined;
   215→
   216→if (!errorStageColumn) {
   217→  sqlite.exec(`ALTER TABLE imports ADD COLUMN error_stage TEXT`);
   218→}
   219→

1→# Testing Best Practices — Chat Exporter
     2→
     3→> Verbindliche Referenz für alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
     4→> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** — es enthält die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.
     5→
     6→## Tech-Stack
     7→
     8→| Bereich | Tool | Version |
     9→|---------|------|---------|
    10→| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
    11→| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
    12→| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
    13→| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | — |
    14→| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |
    15→
    16→## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing
    17→
    18→**Lies nur die Module, die für deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**
    19→
    20→| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
    21→|---------|-------------------|
    22→| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
    23→| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
    24→| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
    25→| **Code-Review durchführen** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
    26→| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
    27→| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
    28→| **Test-Kategorien wählen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
    29→| **Verfügbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gültig)
    34→
    35→### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung
    36→
    37→Tests prüfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.
    38→
    39→```typescript
    40→// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
    41→test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
    42→  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })
    43→
    44→  const result = generatePreview(selection)
    45→
    46→  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
    47→  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
    48→})
    49→
    50→// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
    51→test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
    52→  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
    53→  generatePreview(createSelection())
    54→  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
    55→})
    56→```
    57→
    58→### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)
    59→
    60→Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:
    61→
    62→```typescript
    63→test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
    64→  // Arrange
    65→  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }
    66→
    67→  // Act
    68→  const result = createSelection(overrides)
    69→
    70→  // Assert
    71→  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
    72→  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
    73→  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
    74→})
    75→```
    76→
    77→### Given-When-Then (für komplexe Szenarien)
    78→
    79→```typescript
    80→test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
    81→  /**
    82→   * Given: Eine aktive Session für importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
    83→   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
    84→   * Then: Bestehende Session zurückgegeben, keine neue erstellt
    85→   */
    86→})
    87→```
    88→
    89→### Tests als Dokumentation
    90→
    91→Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
    92→- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
    93→- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
    94→- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)
    99→
   100→| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
   101→|--------------|-------------|
   102→| Implementierungsdetails testen | Prüfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
   103→| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | Prüfe spezifische Werte |
   104→| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo möglich |
   105→| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | Prüfe nur Schema-Konformität |
   106→| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
   107→| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
   108→| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) für Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)
   113→
   114→| Element | Format | Beispiel |
   115→|---------|--------|----------|
   116→| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
   117→| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
   118→| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
   119→| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
   120→| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
   121→| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
   122→| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Detail-Module
   127→
   128→| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
   129→|-------|------|--------|
   130→| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
   131→| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
   132→| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
   133→| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
   134→| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
   135→| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
   136→| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
   137→| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, Ausführungsbefehle |
   138→| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, häufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Änderungen an Test-Praktiken müssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.
   143→

1→# Server-spezifische Test-Patterns
     2→
     3→> Detail-Referenz — geladen aus `TESTING.md` Index.
     4→
     5→## Environment-Variable-Snapshot
     6→
     7→Tests die `process.env` manipulieren, müssen den Originalzustand wiederherstellen:
     8→
     9→```typescript
    10→test("uses custom API key from env", async () => {
    11→  const originalEnv = { ...process.env }
    12→  try {
    13→    process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY = "test-key-123"
    14→
    15→    const result = await createClient()
    16→
    17→    expect(result.apiKey).toBe("test-key-123")
    18→  } finally {
    19→    process.env = originalEnv
    20→  }
    21→}
    22→```
    23→
    24→### Alternativ mit `beforeEach`/`afterEach`
    25→
    26→```typescript
    27→describe("API Client", () => {
    28→  const originalEnv = { ...process.env }
    29→
    30→  afterEach(() => {
    31→    process.env = { ...originalEnv }
    32→  })
    33→
    34→  test("falls back to default when key missing", async () => {
    35→    delete process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
    36→    await expect(createClient()).rejects.toThrow(/API key/i)
    37→  })
    38→})
    39→```
    40→
    41→## Fetch-Mock mit Cleanup
    42→
    43→```typescript
    44→describe("AdjustmentAssistant", () => {
    45→  const originalFetch = globalThis.fetch
    46→
    47→  afterEach(() => {
    48→    globalThis.fetch = originalFetch
    49→  })
    50→
    51→  test("sends structured request to provider", async () => {
    52→    globalThis.fetch = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(
    53→      new Response(JSON.stringify({ choices: [{ message: { content: "result" } }] })),
    54→    )
    55→
    56→    const result = await assistant.process("input")
    57→
    58→    expect(result).toBe("result")
    59→  })
    60→})
    61→```
    62→
    63→## Async-Testing
    64→
    65→Vitest unterstützt async Tests nativ — kein spezielles Plugin nötig:
    66→
    67→```typescript
    68→// Async-Ergebnis prüfen
    69→test("compiles rules asynchronously", async () => {
    70→  const rules = await compileRules(draftRules)
    71→  expect(rules).toHaveLength(3)
    72→})
    73→
    74→// Async-Fehler prüfen
    75→test("rejects invalid rule format", async () => {
    76→  await expect(compileRules(invalidInput)).rejects.toThrow(/invalid selector/i)
    77→})
    78→```
    79→
    80→## Zod-Schema-Validierung testen
    81→
    82→Teste Validierung mit echten Zod-Schemas, nicht Mocks:
    83→
    84→```typescript
    85→import { DraftRuleSchema } from "@chat-exporter/shared"
    86→
    87→test("rejects rule without selector", () => {
    88→  const result = DraftRuleSchema.safeParse({ effect: { type: "replace", value: "x" } })
    89→
    90→  expect(result.success).toBe(false)
    91→  expect(result.error?.issues[0].path).toContain("selector")
    92→})
    93→
    94→test.each(["heading", "paragraph", "code-block"])("accepts valid selector type: %s", (type) => {
    95→  const result = DraftRuleSchema.safeParse({
    96→    selector: { type, value: "test" },
    97→    effect: { type: "replace", value: "changed" },
    98→  })
    99→
   100→  expect(result.success).toBe(true)
   101→})
   102→```
   103→
   104→## Test-Imports
   105→
   106→Server-Tests importieren Vitest-Globals explizit:
   107→
   108→```typescript
   109→import { describe, expect, test } from "vitest"
   110→import type { AdjustmentSelection } from "@chat-exporter/shared"
   111→```
   112→
   113→## Testdatei-Platzierung
   114→
   115→Tests liegen **neben dem Quellcode** (co-located):
   116→
   117→```
   118→apps/server/src/lib/
   119→├── adjustment-preview.ts
   120→├── adjustment-preview.test.ts
   121→├── adjustment-assistant.ts
   122→└── adjustment-assistant.test.ts
   123→```
   124→

Implement Step 1 of the Kontext-Editor plan: Shared Types for Edit- and Snapshot-Schemas.

Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor.
Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`).
Read TESTING.md before writing tests.

## Context

Working directory: /Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter

The shared package is at `packages/shared/src/`. The existing patterns:
- Zod schemas with TypeScript type inference
- Files are named by feature (e.g., `conversation.ts`, `deletions.ts`, `snapshots.ts`)
- Re-exported from `packages/shared/src/index.ts`
- Tests co-located next to source files

Existing Block type from `conversation.ts`:
```typescript
export const blockSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
  paragraphBlockSchema, headingBlockSchema, listBlockSchema,
  codeBlockSchema, quoteBlockSchema, tableBlockSchema,
]);
```

The existing `snapshots.ts` already exists but is about IMPORT snapshots (different concept). The new file should be `edits.ts`.

## What to create

**File: `packages/shared/src/edits.ts` (new)**

1. Zod schema `conversationSnapshotSchema`:
   - `id` (string), `importId` (string), `label` (string), `isActive` (boolean - API level, stored as integer in DB), `createdAt` (string), `updatedAt` (string)

2. Zod schema `messageEditSchema`:
   - `id`, `importId`, `snapshotId`, `messageId`, `annotation` (optional string), `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
   - NO `editedBlocksJson` in API schema (too large, loaded separately in bulk)

3. Request schemas:
   - `saveMessageEditRequestSchema`: `{ importId: string, snapshotId: string, messageId: string, editedBlocks: Block[], annotation?: string }`
   - `deleteMessageEditRequestSchema`: `{ importId: string, snapshotId: string, messageId: string }`
   - `createSnapshotRequestSchema`: `{ importId: string, label: string }`
   - `activateSnapshotRequestSchema`: `{ snapshotId: string }`
   - `renameSnapshotRequestSchema`: `{ snapshotId: string, label: string }`

4. Response schema `resolvedMessageSchema`:
   - `{ messageId: string, blocks: Block[], isEdited: boolean }`

5. TypeScript types derived via z.infer

**File: `packages/shared/src/index.ts` (modify)**
- Add `export * from "./edits.js";`

## Test file: `packages/shared/src/__tests__/edits.test.ts`

Test that:
- Valid payloads pass validation
- Invalid payloads are rejected (missing required fields, wrong types)
- Use `test.each` for testing multiple valid/invalid cases
- Follow AAA pattern

```bash
pnpm --filter @chat-exporter/shared test
pnpm typecheck  # if available, or just tsc
```

toolUseResult.prompt:
Implement Step 2 of the Kontext-Editor plan: DB Schema for new tables conversationSnapshots and messageEdits.

Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor.
Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`).

## Context

Working directory: /Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter

The DB is at `apps/server/src/db/`. Uses:
- `better-sqlite3` with `drizzle-orm`
- Schema definitions in `schema.ts` using `sqliteTable`
- DDL in `client.ts` using `sqlite.exec()`
- Pattern: schema.ts defines Drizzle tables, client.ts has raw CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS

### Existing schema.ts pattern:
```typescript
import { index, sqliteTable, text, uniqueIndex } from "drizzle-orm/sqlite-core";

export const messageDeletions = sqliteTable(
  "message_deletions",
  {
    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    importId: text("import_id").notNull().references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    messageId: text("message_id").notNull(),
    reason: text("reason"),
    deletedAt: text("deleted_at").notNull(),
  },
  (table) => [
    uniqueIndex("idx_message_deletions_import_message").on(table.importId, table.messageId),
  ],
);
```

### Existing client.ts pattern:
DDL statements are added in `sqlite.exec()` block. Add new CREATE TABLE statements.

Also add `integer` import from `drizzle-orm/sqlite-core` for `isActive`.

## What to create

**File: `apps/server/src/db/schema.ts` (modify)**

1. Table `conversationSnapshots`:
   - `id` (text, PK)
   - `importId` (text, FK → imports, CASCADE, NOT NULL)
   - `label` (text, NOT NULL)
   - `isActive` (integer, default 0, NOT NULL) - use `integer("is_active").notNull().default(0)`
   - `createdAt` (text, NOT NULL)
   - `updatedAt` (text, NOT NULL)
   - Index on `importId`

2. Table `messageEdits`:
   - `id` (text, PK)
   - `importId` (text, FK → imports, CASCADE, NOT NULL)
   - `snapshotId` (text, FK → conversationSnapshots, CASCADE, NOT NULL)
   - `messageId` (text, NOT NULL)
   - `editedBlocksJson` (text, NOT NULL)
   - `annotation` (text, nullable)
   - `createdAt` (text, NOT NULL)
   - `updatedAt` (text, NOT NULL)
   - Unique Index on `(snapshotId, messageId)`

3. Export types for both tables

**File: `apps/server/src/db/client.ts` (modify)**

Add DDL statements for both new tables in the `sqlite.exec()` block.

Add `integer` to the drizzle import in schema.ts. Currently it imports: `{ index, sqliteTable, text, uniqueIndex }`.

## Important

- Add the `integer` import from "drizzle-orm/sqlite-core" in schema.ts
- The DDL in client.ts should match the schema.ts definition exactly
- Place new tables after existing tables but before the type exports
- Use the exact same patterns as existing code

## Testing

There are no unit tests for schema.ts - verify by:
1. Running `pnpm typecheck` or the build
2. The server should start without errors

Run: cd "/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter" && pnpm --filter @chat-exporter/server build 2>&1 || pnpm typecheck 2>&1 || echo "Check manually"

```
feat(server): add conversationSnapshots and messageEdits tables

Plan 2.3 Step 2: New DB tables for non-destructive editing.
conversationSnapshots stores named versions per import.
messageEdits stores per-message block overrides per snapshot.

export const messageEdits = sqliteTable(
  "message_edits",
  {
    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    importId: text("import_id").notNull().references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    snapshotId: text("snapshot_id").notNull().references(() => conversationSnapshots.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    messageId: text("message_id").notNull(),
    editedBlocksJson: text("edited_blocks_json").notNull(),
    annotation: text("annotation"),
    createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
    updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
  },

  ],

1. `saveMessageEdit(importId, snapshotId, messageId, editedBlocksJson: string, annotation?: string)`:

   - Use `crypto.randomUUID()` for new ID
   - Return the saved edit record
   
2. `getMessageEdit(snapshotId, messageId)`:
   - Load single edit (with editedBlocksJson)
   - Return the edit or undefined

   - Load all edits for a snapshot (with editedBlocksJson)
   - Return array
   
4. `deleteMessageEdit(snapshotId, messageId)`:
   - Delete edit → message falls back to original
   - Return boolean (whether anything was deleted)
   
5. `deleteAllEditsForSnapshot(snapshotId)`:
   - Delete all edits for a snapshot
   - Return count of deleted edits
   
6. `countEdits(snapshotId)`:

1→# Testing Best Practices — Chat Exporter
     2→
     3→> Verbindliche Referenz für alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
     4→> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** — es enthält die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.
     5→
     6→## Tech-Stack
     7→
     8→| Bereich | Tool | Version |
     9→|---------|------|---------|
    10→| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
    11→| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
    12→| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
    13→| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | — |
    14→| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |
    15→
    16→## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing
    17→
    18→**Lies nur die Module, die für deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**
    19→
    20→| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
    21→|---------|-------------------|
    22→| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
    23→| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
    24→| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
    25→| **Code-Review durchführen** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
    26→| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
    27→| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
    28→| **Test-Kategorien wählen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
    29→| **Verfügbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gültig)
    34→
    35→### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung
    36→
    37→Tests prüfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.
    38→
    39→```typescript
    40→// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
    41→test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
    42→  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })
    43→
    44→  const result = generatePreview(selection)
    45→
    46→  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
    47→  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
    48→})
    49→
    50→// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
    51→test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
    52→  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
    53→  generatePreview(createSelection())
    54→  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
    55→})
    56→```
    57→
    58→### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)
    59→
    60→Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:
    61→
    62→```typescript
    63→test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
    64→  // Arrange
    65→  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }
    66→
    67→  // Act
    68→  const result = createSelection(overrides)
    69→
    70→  // Assert
    71→  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
    72→  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
    73→  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
    74→})
    75→```
    76→
    77→### Given-When-Then (für komplexe Szenarien)
    78→
    79→```typescript
    80→test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
    81→  /**
    82→   * Given: Eine aktive Session für importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
    83→   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
    84→   * Then: Bestehende Session zurückgegeben, keine neue erstellt
    85→   */
    86→})
    87→```
    88→
    89→### Tests als Dokumentation
    90→
    91→Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
    92→- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
    93→- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
    94→- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)
    99→
   100→| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
   101→|--------------|-------------|
   102→| Implementierungsdetails testen | Prüfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
   103→| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | Prüfe spezifische Werte |
   104→| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo möglich |
   105→| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | Prüfe nur Schema-Konformität |
   106→| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
   107→| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
   108→| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) für Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)
   113→
   114→| Element | Format | Beispiel |
   115→|---------|--------|----------|
   116→| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
   117→| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
   118→| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
   119→| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
   120→| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
   121→| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
   122→| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Detail-Module
   127→
   128→| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
   129→|-------|------|--------|
   130→| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
   131→| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
   132→| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
   133→| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
   134→| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
   135→| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
   136→| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
   137→| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, Ausführungsbefehle |
   138→| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, häufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Änderungen an Test-Praktiken müssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.
   143→

1→# Server-spezifische Test-Patterns
     2→
     3→> Detail-Referenz — geladen aus `TESTING.md` Index.
     4→
     5→## Environment-Variable-Snapshot
     6→
     7→Tests die `process.env` manipulieren, müssen den Originalzustand wiederherstellen:
     8→
     9→```typescript
    10→test("uses custom API key from env", async () => {
    11→  const originalEnv = { ...process.env }
    12→  try {
    13→    process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY = "test-key-123"
    14→
    15→    const result = await createClient()
    16→
    17→    expect(result.apiKey).toBe("test-key-123")
    18→  } finally {
    19→    process.env = originalEnv
    20→  }
    21→}
    22→```
    23→
    24→### Alternativ mit `beforeEach`/`afterEach`
    25→
    26→```typescript
    27→describe("API Client", () => {
    28→  const originalEnv = { ...process.env }
    29→
    30→  afterEach(() => {
    31→    process.env = { ...originalEnv }
    32→  })
    33→
    34→  test("falls back to default when key missing", async () => {
    35→    delete process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
    36→    await expect(createClient()).rejects.toThrow(/API key/i)
    37→  })
    38→})
    39→```
    40→
    41→## Fetch-Mock mit Cleanup
    42→
    43→```typescript
    44→describe("AdjustmentAssistant", () => {
    45→  const originalFetch = globalThis.fetch
    46→
    47→  afterEach(() => {
    48→    globalThis.fetch = originalFetch
    49→  })
    50→
    51→  test("sends structured request to provider", async () => {
    52→    globalThis.fetch = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(
    53→      new Response(JSON.stringify({ choices: [{ message: { content: "result" } }] })),
    54→    )
    55→
    56→    const result = await assistant.process("input")
    57→
    58→    expect(result).toBe("result")
    59→  })
    60→})
    61→```
    62→
    63→## Async-Testing
    64→
    65→Vitest unterstützt async Tests nativ — kein spezielles Plugin nötig:
    66→
    67→```typescript
    68→// Async-Ergebnis prüfen
    69→test("compiles rules asynchronously", async () => {
    70→  const rules = await compileRules(draftRules)
    71→  expect(rules).toHaveLength(3)
    72→})
    73→
    74→// Async-Fehler prüfen
    75→test("rejects invalid rule format", async () => {
    76→  await expect(compileRules(invalidInput)).rejects.toThrow(/invalid selector/i)
    77→})
    78→```
    79→
    80→## Zod-Schema-Validierung testen
    81→
    82→Teste Validierung mit echten Zod-Schemas, nicht Mocks:
    83→
    84→```typescript
    85→import { DraftRuleSchema } from "@chat-exporter/shared"
    86→
    87→test("rejects rule without selector", () => {
    88→  const result = DraftRuleSchema.safeParse({ effect: { type: "replace", value: "x" } })
    89→
    90→  expect(result.success).toBe(false)
    91→  expect(result.error?.issues[0].path).toContain("selector")
    92→})
    93→
    94→test.each(["heading", "paragraph", "code-block"])("accepts valid selector type: %s", (type) => {
    95→  const result = DraftRuleSchema.safeParse({
    96→    selector: { type, value: "test" },
    97→    effect: { type: "replace", value: "changed" },
    98→  })
    99→
   100→  expect(result.success).toBe(true)
   101→})
   102→```
   103→
   104→## Test-Imports
   105→
   106→Server-Tests importieren Vitest-Globals explizit:
   107→
   108→```typescript
   109→import { describe, expect, test } from "vitest"
   110→import type { AdjustmentSelection } from "@chat-exporter/shared"
   111→```
   112→
   113→## Testdatei-Platzierung
   114→
   115→Tests liegen **neben dem Quellcode** (co-located):
   116→
   117→```
   118→apps/server/src/lib/
   119→├── adjustment-preview.ts
   120→├── adjustment-preview.test.ts
   121→├── adjustment-assistant.ts
   122→└── adjustment-assistant.test.ts
   123→```
   124→

1→# Fixture-Katalog & Factory-Patterns
     2→
     3→> Detail-Referenz — geladen aus `TESTING.md` Index.
     4→
     5→## Factory-Pattern
     6→
     7→Für Testdaten **Factories mit Defaults + Overrides** verwenden — resilient gegen Schema-Änderungen:
     8→
     9→```typescript
    10→import type { AdjustmentSelection } from "@chat-exporter/shared"
    11→
    12→function createSelection(overrides: Partial<AdjustmentSelection> = {}): AdjustmentSelection {
    13→  return {
    14→    blockIndex: 0,
    15→    blockType: "paragraph",
    16→    messageId: "message-1",
    17→    messageIndex: 0,
    18→    messageRole: "assistant",
    19→    selectedText: "Example content",
    20→    textQuote: "Example content",
    21→    ...overrides,
    22→  }
    23→}
    24→```
    25→
    26→### Regeln für Factories
    27→
    28→- **Spread-Overrides** statt optionale Parameter — flexibler bei Schema-Erweiterungen
    29→- **Minimale Defaults** — nur Pflichtfelder, optionale Felder weglassen
    30→- **Typisiert** — `Partial<T>` für Overrides, Rückgabetyp explizit
    31→
    32→## Bestehende Factories (Server)
    33→
    34→| Factory | Datei | Beschreibung |
    35→|---------|-------|-------------|
    36→| `createSelection(overrides?)` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` | `AdjustmentSelection` mit Defaults |
    37→| `createSession(overrides?)` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` | `AdjustmentSessionDetail` mit Messages |
    38→| `createImportJob(overrides?)` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` | `ImportJob` mit Defaults |
    39→
    40→## E2E-Fixtures (Playwright)
    41→
    42→E2E-Tests nutzen Playwrights Fixture-System für Page Objects:
    43→
    44→```typescript
    45→// e2e/fixtures/test-fixtures.ts
    46→import { test as base, expect } from "@playwright/test"
    47→import { HomePage } from "../pages/home.page"
    48→import { FormatWorkspacePage } from "../pages/format-workspace.page"
    49→
    50→interface TestFixtures {
    51→  homePage: HomePage
    52→  workspace: FormatWorkspacePage
    53→}
    54→
    55→export const test = base.extend<TestFixtures>({
    56→  homePage: async ({ page }, use) => {
    57→    await use(new HomePage(page))
    58→  },
    59→  workspace: async ({ page }, use) => {
    60→    await use(new FormatWorkspacePage(page))
    61→  },
    62→})
    63→
    64→export { expect } from "@playwright/test"
    65→```
    66→
    67→### Bestehende E2E-Fixtures
    68→
    69→| Fixture | Typ | Beschreibung |
    70→|---------|-----|-------------|
    71→| `homePage` | `HomePage` | Navigation, URL-Import |
    72→| `workspace` | `FormatWorkspacePage` | Format-Workspace-Interaktionen |
    73→| `FIXTURE_IMPORT_ID` | `string` | Seeder-Import-ID für Tests (`"smoke-format-adjustments-v1"`) |
    74→
    75→## Neue Factory erstellen — Checkliste
    76→
    77→1. Shared-Type aus `@chat-exporter/shared` importieren
    78→2. Factory neben den Tests definieren (oder in geteilte Datei wenn >2 Tests sie nutzen)
    79→3. `Partial<T>` für Overrides
    80→4. Nur Pflichtfelder als Defaults
    81→5. Spread-Merge: `{ ...defaults, ...overrides }`
    82→

1→import {
     2→  index,
     3→  integer,
     4→  sqliteTable,

     6→  uniqueIndex,
     7→} from "drizzle-orm/sqlite-core";
     8→
     9→export const imports = sqliteTable(
    10→  "imports",
    11→  {
    12→    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    13→    sourceUrl: text("source_url").notNull(),
    14→    sourcePlatform: text("source_platform").notNull(),
    15→    mode: text("mode").notNull(),
    16→    status: text("status").notNull(),
    17→    currentStage: text("current_stage").notNull(),
    18→    createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
    19→    updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
    20→    warningsJson: text("warnings_json").notNull().default("[]"),
    21→    error: text("error"),
    22→    errorStage: text("error_stage"),
    23→    summaryJson: text("summary_json"),
    24→    conversationJson: text("conversation_json"),
    25→    artifactsJson: text("artifacts_json"),
    26→  },
    27→  (table) => [
    28→    index("idx_imports_created_at").on(table.createdAt),
    29→    index("idx_imports_status").on(table.status),
    30→  ],
    31→);
    32→
    33→export const importSnapshots = sqliteTable("import_snapshots", {
    34→  importId: text("import_id")
    35→    .primaryKey()
    36→    .references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    37→  sourceUrl: text("source_url").notNull(),
    38→  finalUrl: text("final_url").notNull(),
    39→  fetchedAt: text("fetched_at").notNull(),
    40→  pageTitle: text("page_title").notNull(),
    41→  rawHtml: text("raw_html").notNull(),
    42→  normalizedPayloadJson: text("normalized_payload_json").notNull(),
    43→  fetchMetadataJson: text("fetch_metadata_json").notNull(),
    44→  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
    45→});
    46→
    47→export const adjustmentSessions = sqliteTable(
    48→  "adjustment_sessions",
    49→  {
    50→    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    51→    importId: text("import_id")
    52→      .notNull()
    53→      .references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    54→    targetFormat: text("target_format").notNull(),
    55→    status: text("status").notNull(),
    56→    selectionJson: text("selection_json").notNull(),
    57→    previewArtifactJson: text("preview_artifact_json"),
    58→    createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
    59→    updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
    60→  },
    61→  (table) => [
    62→    index("idx_adjustment_sessions_import_id").on(
    63→      table.importId,
    64→      table.createdAt,
    65→    ),
    66→    index("idx_adjustment_sessions_target_format").on(
    67→      table.targetFormat,
    68→      table.updatedAt,
    69→    ),
    70→  ],
    71→);
    72→
    73→export const adjustmentMessages = sqliteTable(
    74→  "adjustment_messages",
    75→  {
    76→    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    77→    sessionId: text("session_id")
    78→      .notNull()
    79→      .references(() => adjustmentSessions.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    80→    role: text("role").notNull(),
    81→    content: text("content").notNull(),
    82→    createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
    83→  },
    84→  (table) => [
    85→    index("idx_adjustment_messages_session_id").on(
    86→      table.sessionId,
    87→      table.createdAt,
    88→    ),
    89→  ],
    90→);
    91→
    92→export const formatRules = sqliteTable(
    93→  "format_rules",
    94→  {
    95→    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    96→    importId: text("import_id").references(() => imports.id, {
    97→      onDelete: "cascade",
    98→    }),
    99→    targetFormat: text("target_format").notNull(),
   100→    kind: text("kind").notNull(),
   101→    scope: text("scope").notNull(),
   102→    status: text("status").notNull(),
   103→    selectorJson: text("selector_json").notNull(),
   104→    instruction: text("instruction").notNull(),
   105→    compiledRuleJson: text("compiled_rule_json"),
   106→    sourceSessionId: text("source_session_id").references(
   107→      () => adjustmentSessions.id,
   108→      { onDelete: "set null" },
   109→    ),
   110→    createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
   111→    updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
   112→  },
   113→  (table) => [
   114→    index("idx_format_rules_import_id").on(
   115→      table.importId,
   116→      table.targetFormat,
   117→      table.status,
   118→      table.createdAt,
   119→    ),
   120→    index("idx_format_rules_profile").on(
   121→      table.targetFormat,
   122→      table.status,
   123→      table.createdAt,
   124→    ),
   125→  ],
   126→);
   127→
   128→export const adjustmentEvents = sqliteTable(
   129→  "adjustment_events",
   130→  {
   131→    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
   132→    importId: text("import_id")
   133→      .notNull()
   134→      .references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   135→    sessionId: text("session_id").references(() => adjustmentSessions.id, {
   136→      onDelete: "set null",
   137→    }),
   138→    ruleId: text("rule_id").references(() => formatRules.id, {
   139→      onDelete: "set null",
   140→    }),
   141→    targetFormat: text("target_format").notNull(),
   142→    eventType: text("event_type").notNull(),
   143→    payloadJson: text("payload_json"),
   144→    createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
   145→  },
   146→  (table) => [
   147→    index("idx_adjustment_events_import_id").on(
   148→      table.importId,
   149→      table.targetFormat,
   150→      table.createdAt,
   151→    ),
   152→    index("idx_adjustment_events_session_id").on(
   153→      table.sessionId,
   154→      table.createdAt,
   155→    ),
   156→  ],
   157→);
   158→
   159→export const messageDeletions = sqliteTable(
   160→  "message_deletions",
   161→  {
   162→    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
   163→    importId: text("import_id")
   164→      .notNull()
   165→      .references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   166→    messageId: text("message_id").notNull(),
   167→    reason: text("reason"),
   168→    deletedAt: text("deleted_at").notNull(),
   169→  },
   170→  (table) => [
   171→    uniqueIndex("idx_message_deletions_import_message").on(
   172→      table.importId,
   173→      table.messageId,
   174→    ),
   175→  ],
   176→);
   177→
   178→export const conversationSnapshots = sqliteTable(
   179→  "conversation_snapshots",
   180→  {
   181→    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
   182→    importId: text("import_id")
   183→      .notNull()
   184→      .references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   185→    label: text("label").notNull(),
   186→    isActive: integer("is_active").notNull().default(0),
   187→    createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
   188→    updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
   189→  },
   190→  (table) => [index("idx_conversation_snapshots_import_id").on(table.importId)],
   191→);
   192→
   193→export const messageEdits = sqliteTable(
   194→  "message_edits",
   195→  {
   196→    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
   197→    importId: text("import_id")
   198→      .notNull()
   199→      .references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   200→    snapshotId: text("snapshot_id")
   201→      .notNull()
   202→      .references(() => conversationSnapshots.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   203→    messageId: text("message_id").notNull(),
   204→    editedBlocksJson: text("edited_blocks_json").notNull(),
   205→    annotation: text("annotation"),
   206→    createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
   207→    updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
   208→  },
   209→  (table) => [
   210→    uniqueIndex("idx_message_edits_snapshot_message").on(
   211→      table.snapshotId,
   212→      table.messageId,
   213→    ),
   214→  ],
   215→);
   216→
   217→export type Import = typeof imports.$inferSelect;
   218→export type NewImport = typeof imports.$inferInsert;
   219→export type ImportSnapshot = typeof importSnapshots.$inferSelect;
   220→export type NewImportSnapshot = typeof importSnapshots.$inferInsert;
   221→export type AdjustmentSessionRecord = typeof adjustmentSessions.$inferSelect;
   222→export type AdjustmentMessageRecord = typeof adjustmentMessages.$inferSelect;
   223→export type FormatRuleRecord = typeof formatRules.$inferSelect;
   224→export type AdjustmentEventRecord = typeof adjustmentEvents.$inferSelect;
   225→export type MessageDeletionRecord = typeof messageDeletions.$inferSelect;
   226→export type ConversationSnapshotRecord =
   227→  typeof conversationSnapshots.$inferSelect;
   228→export type NewConversationSnapshot = typeof conversationSnapshots.$inferInsert;
   229→export type MessageEditRecord = typeof messageEdits.$inferSelect;
   230→export type NewMessageEdit = typeof messageEdits.$inferInsert;

Implement Step 4 of the Kontext-Editor plan: Snapshot Repository CRUD.

Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor.
Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`).
Read TESTING.md before writing tests.

## Context

Working directory: /Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter

The repositories are at `apps/server/src/lib/`. Pattern from `delete-repository.ts`:
```typescript
import type { MessageDeletion } from "@chat-exporter/shared";
import { and, eq } from "drizzle-orm";
import { db, withTransaction } from "../db/client.js";
import { imports, messageDeletions } from "../db/schema.js";
```

DB Schema (just added in schema.ts):
```typescript
export const conversationSnapshots = sqliteTable(
  "conversation_snapshots",
  {
    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    importId: text("import_id").notNull().references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    label: text("label").notNull(),
    isActive: integer("is_active").notNull().default(0),
    createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
    updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
  },
  (table) => [
    index("idx_conversation_snapshots_import_id").on(table.importId),
  ],
);
```

Shared types from `@chat-exporter/shared`:
- `ConversationSnapshot` = { id, importId, label, isActive: boolean, createdAt, updatedAt }

Note: DB stores isActive as integer (0/1), API uses boolean. Convert in repository layer.

## What to create

**File: `apps/server/src/lib/snapshot-repository.ts` (new)**

Functions:
1. `createSnapshot(importId: string, label: string)`: 
   - Create new snapshot with isActive=0
   - Use `crypto.randomUUID()` for ID
   - Return the created snapshot (with isActive as boolean)

2. `listSnapshots(importId: string)`:
   - All snapshots for import, ordered by createdAt
   - Return with isActive as boolean

3. `getActiveSnapshot(importId: string)`:
   - Return the active snapshot or null
   - isActive as boolean

4. `activateSnapshot(snapshotId: string)`:
   - In transaction: set all snapshots of same import to isActive=0, then set this one to isActive=1
   - Need to first get the snapshot to find importId
   - Return updated snapshot

5. `deactivateAllSnapshots(importId: string)`:
   - Set all snapshots of import to isActive=0
   - Wrap in transaction

6. `deleteSnapshot(snapshotId: string)`:
   - Delete snapshot (CASCADE will delete associated messageEdits)
   - Return boolean

7. `renameSnapshot(snapshotId: string, label: string)`:
   - Update label and updatedAt
   - Return updated snapshot

Helper: `toApiSnapshot(row)` that converts DB row (isActive: 0|1) to API format (isActive: boolean).

Use `db` and `withTransaction` from `../db/client.js`.
Use `conversationSnapshots` from `../db/schema.js`.
Use `eq`, `and`, `ne` from `drizzle-orm` as needed.

## Test file: `apps/server/src/lib/snapshot-repository.test.ts`

Look at existing test files first: `ls apps/server/src/lib/*.test.ts` and `ls apps/server/src/db/*.test.ts` to see how they handle DB setup for repository tests.

Test cases:
- Create snapshot and verify fields
- List snapshots for import (empty and non-empty)
- getActiveSnapshot returns null when none active
- Activate snapshot deactivates all others of same import
- Deactivate all snapshots
- Delete snapshot returns true (and CASCADE deletes edits if any)
- Rename snapshot updates label and updatedAt
- isActive is boolean in API (not integer)

You'll need to insert a test import first since conversationSnapshots references imports(id). Create a minimal import record.

Run tests: cd "/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter" && pnpm --filter @chat-exporter/server test

Commit message:
```
feat(server): add snapshot repository with CRUD operations

Plan 2.3 Step 4: Repository for conversation snapshots with create,
list, activate/deactivate, delete, and rename operations.
```

   170→// Migration: make format_rules.import_id nullable (SQLite cannot ALTER COLUMN)
   171→// Uses a table-rebuild approach because SQLite does not support DROP NOT NULL.
   172→const importIdColumn = sqlite
   173→  .prepare(
   174→    `SELECT "notnull" FROM pragma_table_info('format_rules') WHERE name = 'import_id'`,
   175→  )
   176→  .get() as { notnull: number } | undefined;
   177→
   178→if (importIdColumn && importIdColumn.notnull === 1) {
   179→  // Per SQLite docs, PRAGMA foreign_keys is a no-op inside a transaction, so it
   180→  // must be issued outside one. We therefore split the migration into three
   181→  // separate sqlite.exec calls:
   182→  //   1. Pragma off  (outside transaction)
   183→  //   2. DDL block wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT — crash-safe: a failure between
   184→  //      DROP TABLE and RENAME rolls back automatically, preserving all data.
   185→  //   3. Pragma on   (outside transaction)
   186→  sqlite.exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF;");
   187→
   188→  sqlite.exec(`
   189→    BEGIN;
   190→
   191→    -- Create the new table with nullable import_id
   192→    CREATE TABLE format_rules_new (
   193→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   194→      import_id TEXT REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   195→      target_format TEXT NOT NULL,
   196→      kind TEXT NOT NULL,
   197→      scope TEXT NOT NULL,
   198→      status TEXT NOT NULL,
   199→      selector_json TEXT NOT NULL,
   200→      instruction TEXT NOT NULL,
   201→      compiled_rule_json TEXT,
   202→      source_session_id TEXT REFERENCES adjustment_sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   203→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   204→      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
   205→    );
   206→
   207→    -- Copy data from old table to new
   208→    INSERT INTO format_rules_new SELECT * FROM format_rules;
   209→
   210→    -- Drop original and rename new to final name
   211→    DROP TABLE format_rules;
   212→    ALTER TABLE format_rules_new RENAME TO format_rules;
   213→
   214→    -- Recreate indexes
   215→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_import_id
   216→      ON format_rules (import_id, target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   217→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_profile
   218→      ON format_rules (target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   219→
   220→    COMMIT;
   221→  `);
   222→
   223→  sqlite.exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;");
   224→
   225→  // Verify FK integrity after migration
   226→  const fkErrors = sqlite.pragma("foreign_key_check") as unknown[];
   227→  if (fkErrors.length > 0) {
   228→    throw new Error(
   229→      `FK-Integritätsprüfung nach Migration fehlgeschlagen: ${JSON.stringify(fkErrors)}`,
   230→    );
   231→  }
   232→}
   233→
   234→// Migration: add error_stage column to imports table
   235→// Note: sqlite.exec is the better-sqlite3 DDL API — no shell invocation.
   236→const errorStageColumn = sqlite
   237→  .prepare(

   239→  )
   240→  .get() as { name: string } | undefined;

   251→  )
   252→  .get() as { name: string } | undefined;

   263→      label TEXT NOT NULL,
   264→      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   265→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   266→      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
   267→    );
   268→
   269→    INSERT INTO conversation_snapshots_new (id, import_id, label, is_active, created_at, updated_at)
   270→      SELECT id, import_id, label, CAST(is_active AS INTEGER), created_at, updated_at
   271→      FROM conversation_snapshots;
   272→
   273→    DROP TABLE conversation_snapshots;
   274→    ALTER TABLE conversation_snapshots_new RENAME TO conversation_snapshots;
   275→
   276→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_conversation_snapshots_import_id
   277→      ON conversation_snapshots (import_id);
   278→
   279→    COMMIT;
   280→  `);

1→# Testing Best Practices — Chat Exporter
     2→
     3→> Verbindliche Referenz für alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
     4→> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** — es enthält die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.
     5→
     6→## Tech-Stack
     7→
     8→| Bereich | Tool | Version |
     9→|---------|------|---------|
    10→| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
    11→| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
    12→| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
    13→| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | — |
    14→| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |
    15→
    16→## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing
    17→
    18→**Lies nur die Module, die für deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**
    19→
    20→| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
    21→|---------|-------------------|
    22→| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
    23→| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
    24→| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
    25→| **Code-Review durchführen** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
    26→| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
    27→| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
    28→| **Test-Kategorien wählen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
    29→| **Verfügbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gültig)
    34→
    35→### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung
    36→
    37→Tests prüfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.
    38→
    39→```typescript
    40→// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
    41→test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
    42→  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })
    43→
    44→  const result = generatePreview(selection)
    45→
    46→  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
    47→  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
    48→})
    49→
    50→// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
    51→test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
    52→  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
    53→  generatePreview(createSelection())
    54→  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
    55→})
    56→```
    57→
    58→### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)
    59→
    60→Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:
    61→
    62→```typescript
    63→test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
    64→  // Arrange
    65→  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }
    66→
    67→  // Act
    68→  const result = createSelection(overrides)
    69→
    70→  // Assert
    71→  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
    72→  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
    73→  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
    74→})
    75→```
    76→
    77→### Given-When-Then (für komplexe Szenarien)
    78→
    79→```typescript
    80→test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
    81→  /**
    82→   * Given: Eine aktive Session für importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
    83→   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
    84→   * Then: Bestehende Session zurückgegeben, keine neue erstellt
    85→   */
    86→})
    87→```
    88→
    89→### Tests als Dokumentation
    90→
    91→Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
    92→- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
    93→- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
    94→- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)
    99→
   100→| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
   101→|--------------|-------------|
   102→| Implementierungsdetails testen | Prüfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
   103→| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | Prüfe spezifische Werte |
   104→| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo möglich |
   105→| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | Prüfe nur Schema-Konformität |
   106→| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
   107→| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
   108→| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) für Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)
   113→
   114→| Element | Format | Beispiel |
   115→|---------|--------|----------|
   116→| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
   117→| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
   118→| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
   119→| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
   120→| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
   121→| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
   122→| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Detail-Module
   127→
   128→| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
   129→|-------|------|--------|
   130→| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
   131→| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
   132→| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
   133→| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
   134→| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
   135→| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
   136→| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
   137→| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, Ausführungsbefehle |
   138→| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, häufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Änderungen an Test-Praktiken müssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.
   143→
